meta.name: World War II - 1939
meta.author: Historia
meta.description: September 1, 1939. Germany invades Poland. The world plunges into the deadliest conflict in human history.

ruler.name: Ruler of Tanzania, United Republic of

armies.name: Army of Tanzania, United Republic of

ruler.name: Ruler of Union of South Africa

armies.name: Army of Union of South Africa

nodes.name: German Invasion of Poland
nodes.description: Germany launches a Blitzkrieg attack on Poland, starting WWII. Poland is partitioned between Germany and the USSR.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the German Blitzkrieg invasion of Poland in September 1939.

nodes.name: Winter War
nodes.description: The Soviet Union invades Finland. The Finns mount a fierce resistance but are eventually forced to cede territory.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Soviet invasion of Finland and the Finns' fierce winter resistance.

nodes.name: Fall of France
nodes.description: Germany conquers France in a swift six-week campaign through the Ardennes. France signs an armistice.

preventionConditions.description: France strengthens its defenses beyond the Maginot Line

nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the fall of France as German Panzer divisions smash through the Ardennes, bypassing the Maginot Line.

nodes.name: Battle of Britain
nodes.description: The Luftwaffe attempts to gain air superiority over Britain. The RAF prevails.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Battle of Britain — the RAF's finest hour defending against the Luftwaffe onslaught.

nodes.name: Operation Barbarossa
nodes.description: Germany invades the Soviet Union with 3 million troops, the largest military invasion in history.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe Operation Barbarossa — the largest military invasion in history.

nodes.name: Attack on Pearl Harbor
nodes.description: Japan attacks the US Pacific Fleet, bringing America into the war.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Japanese surprise attack on Pearl Harbor.

nodes.name: Battle of Stalingrad
nodes.description: The turning point of the Eastern Front. The German 6th Army is encircled and destroyed at Stalingrad.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Battle of Stalingrad — the bloodiest battle in human history and the turning point of WWII.

nodes.name: D-Day — Normandy Landings
nodes.description: Allied forces land in Normandy, opening a second front in Western Europe.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe D-Day — the greatest amphibious invasion in history.

nodes.name: Fall of Berlin
nodes.description: Soviet forces storm Berlin. Germany surrenders unconditionally, ending the war in Europe.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the fall of Berlin and the end of the Third Reich.

nodes.name: Atomic Bombings of Japan
nodes.description: The United States drops atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki. Japan surrenders, ending WWII.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the atomic bombings and Japan's unconditional surrender.

narrative.introduction: September 1, 1939. At dawn, German forces crossed the Polish border. The world holds its breath as the greatest conflict in human history begins.
